title: Rootless build: cannot specify gid= mount options for unmapped gid in rootless containers
body:
> /kind bug

*Note: I'm unsure whether this is a bug in buildah or podman. Please tell if I should file another bug report against buildah.*

**Description**

Consider this simple Dockerfile:
```
FROM fedora:28
RUN dnf -y update
```

Using `podman --log-level=debug build .` fails with `cannot specify gid= mount options for unmapped gid in rootless containers`, [logs here](https://home.in.tum.de/~engelke/raw/podman.3.log). (Note: the logs are copy-pasted from the terminal, if I redirect stderr, podman just hangs right before the error.)

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

See above.

**Describe the results you received:**

`cannot specify gid= mount options for unmapped gid in rootless containers`

**Describe the results you expected:**

A successful build.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:       0.7.2
Go Version:    go1.10.3
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```
<snip>
registries:
  registries:
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - quay.io
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - registry.centos.org
store:
  ContainerStore:
    number: 4
  GraphDriverName: vfs
  GraphOptions: []
  GraphRoot: /var/home/engelke/.local/share/containers/storage
  GraphStatus: {}
  ImageStore:
    number: 2
  RunRoot: /run/user/1000/run
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Operating system if Fedora Atomic Workstation `28.20180723.0`.

comments.body:
@aengelke Thanks for the problem report.  We'll take a look at it.  Adding an issue here in Podman or in Buildah works either way.

A couple of questions for you to make sure I'm on the same page.  When you ran the `podman --log-level=debug build .` you were just on the Fedora Atomic Workstation right?  You hadn't created a container and were trying to do the podman command from within the container?

Can you drop the `dnf -y update` from the Dockerfile?  Does that succeed?  If so, could you try:
`podman --log-level=debug build -t myfed .` and then `podman run myfed dnf -y update` and let me know if that works?
Have we tested how Podman's rootless userns code works with buildah? Should we disable the Podman rootless code once Buildah has its own working rootless support?
Disable the Podman code just for `podman build` I mean - so we don't have potentially clashing rootless implementations?

If podman build does not work yet in rootless mode, we should probably exit early.  But the rootless stuff is very experimental at this point.
@giuseppe Can you take a look.

@TomSweeneyRedHat Yes, this is native FAW, no nested containers. If I drop the `RUN` from the Dockerfile, the build succeeds just fine. Also running `podman run --network=host myfed dnf -y update` afterwards works as intended. (Without the network option, the container has no network access (which is probably intended?).)

@aengelke Podman presently has better rootless support than Buildah, which is probably why the `RUN` line works in Podman but not `podman build`. Network support without --host is actually being worked on - @giuseppe is looking into it.
Cool, thanks!
Dang @aengelke you beat me to the punch.  I was just about to write up a note saying I'd tested and found out exactly what you wrote.  FWIW, the command runs fine if you run as root.

I *think* that is a bug in runc.  It should not barf if we are already in an user namespace but we don't specify UID/GID mappings in the OCI spec file.  I've already fixed a similar issue in runc (https://github.com/opencontainers/runc/commit/cbcc85d311725031e5957385f3ad43acfc0b66f2) and it is probably a similar one.  To avoid these kind of issues altogether in podman I've added this code ed0261176b2bd9ccd9a6cbf77727429429b8fedc that is probably missing in Buildah.

About the rootless network support: me and @AkihiroSuda decided to collaborate on https://github.com/AkihiroSuda/slirp4netns as I was working on a similar project.  We are discussing about moving it to https://github.com/rootless-containers/ but we will need a way to distribute it in Fedora (new package?) so that it can be used from our tools to setup the network

FWIW, I tried the build using Buildah by itself as a non-root user.  It didn't complete there either, but made a lot more progress.  It failed in the commit process.  The `RUN dnf -y update` command completed successfully in Buildah.  I've logged an error at:  https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/issues/882.

I think the issue will be fixed via "Step 2" of https://github.com/opencontainers/runc/issues/1837
@giuseppe PTAL?
@AkihiroSuda yes, I guess so.  Altough, it will probably easier for now to drop that check in runc since it cannot work correctly in all situations.

W.r.t. https://github.com/opencontainers/runc/issues/1837 , maybe I should close the "Step 1" PR and go to "Step 2" directly?
cc @cyphar
Yeah, I think splitting up the rootless mode is probably the best way of dealing with this.

:+1:, PTAL the Step2 POC? https://github.com/AkihiroSuda/runc/commit/eabcf365351f55af951af2dbeb0822efe52c4379

If SGTY I'll open a PR
@cyphar @giuseppe PTAL the "Step 2" code?
BTW if review for "Step 2" takes some time, it would be great if the "Step 1" can get merged now
@AkihiroSuda @giuseppe What is the state of this issue?
https://github.com/opencontainers/runc/pull/1862 should fix the issue, ptal

Looks like thiere was a fix for this merged months ago
@aengelke Reopen this issue if the problem still exists.
